items.prompt: computes simple and compound probabilities and uses organized models to solve bounded chance problems
items.expected_evidence: compute and interpret probabilities for simple and compound events in bounded sample spaces
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner builds a correct event model and computes probabilities consistent with that structure.

items.prompt: explains how event structure, independence, and conditioning affect the probability of a compound event
items.expected_evidence: explain independence, dependence, and introductory conditional probability in context
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains why the probability rule fits the event structure, not just the final number.

items.prompt: Diagnose a peer error aligned to overlap_double_counting.
items.expected_evidence: represent compound events with organized models such as tables, trees, or set diagrams
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies where overlapping outcomes were counted incorrectly or events were modeled inconsistently.

items.prompt: compares experimental and theoretical probabilities in a less-familiar repeated-trials context and judges whether the variation is plausible
items.expected_evidence: compare experimental results to theoretical models and explain variation without overreacting to short-run noise
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves model meaning while interpreting trial-to-trial variation realistically.
